audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for miraculous catch, calling Simon/James/John, cleansing of skin disease, paralyzed man, Levi call, table fellowship, fasting, and new wine/wineskins.

verses.restored: It happened as the crowd pressed upon him to hear the word of the Divine, he was standing beside the lake of Gennesaret.
He saw two boats standing beside the lake; the fishers had gone out from them and were washing the nets.
Entering one of the boats, which was Simon's, he asked him to put out a little from the land; sitting down, he taught the crowds from the boat.
When he stopped speaking, he said to Simon, Put out into the deep and let down your nets for a catch.
Simon answered, Master, through the whole night we labored and took nothing; but at your word I will let down the nets.
Doing this, they enclosed a great multitude of fish, and their nets were breaking.
They signaled to partners in the other boat to come help them; they came and filled both boats, so that they were sinking.
Seeing this, Simon Peter fell at Jesus' knees, saying, Depart from me, Lord, because I am a sinful/misaligned man.
For astonishment seized him and all those with him at the catch of fish they had taken.
Likewise also James and John, sons of Zebedee, who were partners with Simon. Jesus said to Simon, Do not fear; from now you will be catching people alive.
Bringing the boats to land, leaving everything, they followed him.
While he was in one of the cities, look, a man full of skin disease. Seeing Jesus, he fell on his face and pleaded, Lord, if you are willing, you are able to cleanse me.
Stretching out his hand, he touched him, saying, I am willing; be cleansed. Immediately the skin disease left him.
He instructed him to tell no one, but, Go show yourself to the priest and offer for your cleansing as Moses commanded, as a testimony to them.
The word about him spread even more, and great crowds came together to hear and to be healed from their weaknesses.
But he would withdraw in deserted places and pray.

On one of the days he was teaching, Pharisees and teachers of the Law were sitting there, who had come from every village of Galilee and Judea and from Jerusalem; and power of the Lord was with him to heal.
Look, men were carrying on a bed a person who was paralyzed, and they were seeking to bring him in and place him before Jesus.
Not finding how to bring him in because of the crowd, they went up on the roof and lowered him through the tiles with the bed into the middle before Jesus.
Seeing their trust, he said, Human one, your sins/misalignments are released for you.
The scribes and Pharisees began reasoning, saying, Who is this who speaks blasphemies? Who is able to release sins/misalignments except the Divine alone?
Jesus, knowing their reasonings, answered them, Why are you reasoning in your hearts?
Which is easier: to say, Your sins/misalignments are released for you, or to say, Rise and walk?
But so that you may know that the Son of Humanity has authority on earth to release sins/misalignments, he said to the paralyzed one, I say to you, rise, take up your bed, and go to your house.
Immediately rising before them, he took up what he had been lying on and went to his house, glorifying the Divine.
Astonishment seized them all; they glorified the Divine and were filled with fear, saying, We have seen remarkable things today.
After these things he went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at the tax booth, and he said to him, Follow me.
Leaving everything, he rose and followed him.
Levi made a great feast for him in his house, and there was a great crowd of tax collectors and others reclining with them.
The Pharisees and their scribes were grumbling to his disciples, saying, Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners/misaligned ones?
Jesus answered them, Those who are healthy do not need a physician, but those having harm.
I have not come to call righteous ones but sinners/misaligned ones into turning/realignment.
They said to him, John's disciples fast often and make prayers, likewise also those of the Pharisees, but yours eat and drink.
Jesus said to them, Can you make the sons of the bridal chamber fast while the bridegroom is with them?
Days will come, and when the bridegroom is taken away from them, then they will fast in those days.
He also spoke a parable to them: No one tears a patch from a new garment and puts it on an old garment; otherwise the new will be torn, and the patch from the new will not match the old.
No one puts new wine into old wineskins; otherwise the new wine will burst the skins, and it will be spilled, and the skins destroyed.
But new wine must be put into fresh wineskins.
And no one drinking old wine desires new, for he says, The old is good/useful.
